user_query:
I'm hiring you to set up an AI-assisted development workflow for our small team building an internal "Release Readiness Dashboard" service. Please read all files under input/ before you start:
- input/project-brief.md
- input/existing-structure.json
- input/ambiguous-decision.md
- input/nonfunctional-requirements.yaml
- input/security-checklist.md

Deliverables (write all outputs under output/):

1) output/PROJECT_RULES.md — A durable rules file for this codebase with clearly labeled sections: Stack, Code Style, Architecture, Testing, Do NOT, When Unsure. Include explicit limits (max function length and max file length), a dependency policy (no new deps without approval), error handling conventions, naming conventions, and security directives (no hardcoded secrets, input validation expectations). Keep it generic (no tool or vendor names).

2) output/RPIV_PLAN.md — Follow Research → Plan → Implement → Validate. Requirements:
   - Include a "Decision Matrix" scoring for whether vibe coding is appropriate (score must be 0–6) with justification tied to the project.
   - Research: Summarize what you learned from each input file explicitly (cite each by name) and note assumptions/unknowns.
   - Plan: List files to create/modify, the order of changes, and risks/unknowns.
   - Implement: Break work into steps that respect the 200-line rule (state this explicitly) and include checkpoints.
   - Validate: Define tests and manual verification steps tied to acceptance criteria from the inputs.

3) output/DESIGN_DECISION.md — Explore the architectural decision in input/ambiguous-decision.md by generating 3–4 distinct solution approaches. For each, include a "Universe" section with core idea, architecture sketch, pros, cons/risks, and when it’s most suitable. Include a comparison matrix, then a "Decision" section explaining what you chose (or a hybrid) and why, and an "Implementation plan" for the chosen path.

4) output/TEST_STRATEGY.md — Define a testing strategy mapped to this project using the testing pyramid with percentage targets, what to test at each layer, and a test-first outline for at least one critical module. Include guidance on minimizing brittle tests and how to enforce coverage thresholds.

5) output/PRODUCTION_CHECKLIST.md — Assess production readiness against P0 (Security), P1 (Performance), P2 (Reliability), and P3 (Quality). For each item, mark pass/fail/n/a and write concrete fixes with file-level pointers for any failures.

Constraints:
- No new dependencies without an explicit policy in the rules file.
- Keep files under a 300-line limit and functions under your specified max length.
- Types-first and small-file architecture.
- Do not reference specific external tools or vendors; keep everything text-based and framework-agnostic.

Please proceed and produce the five files above.
